pi_items.source: Drupal Commerce promotion naming conventions confirmed via official documentation; task specification consistent with established patterns (e.g., pre-loaded 'Electronics 15% Off' promotion in setup_drupal_commerce.sh line 535)

Valid coupon code format verified via Drupal Commerce source code (commerce/modules/promotion/src/Entity/Coupon.php); matches pattern of pre-loaded coupons (WELCOME10, SAVE25) referenced in setup_drupal_commerce.sh line 535

Drupal Commerce documentation at https://docs.drupalcommerce.org/v2/user-guide/promotions/ confirms 'Percentage off the subtotal' as a standard offer type; verified in source code with offer plugin IDs like 'order_item_percentage_off'

Consistent with promotion name specification; task requires 20% off the order subtotal per task.json line 5; percentage values in Drupal Commerce are stored as numeric strings representing the percentage

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a synthetic promotion in a local Drupal Commerce environment (Docker-based MariaDB database). The metadata specifies creation of a promotion named 'Summer Sale 20% Off' with a percentage-based discount of 20% and an associated coupon code 'SUMMER20'. All metadata values are verified as realistic and consistent with Drupal Commerce 2.x specifications: percentage discounts are a standard offer type supported by the commerce_promotion module, coupon codes follow valid alphanumeric naming patterns consistent with pre-loaded examples, and promotion names follow established conventions. The promotion will be stored in the commerce_promotion_field_data table and the coupon in the commerce_promotion_coupon table within the local MariaDB environment.
